← the Atlas

awarenessd — terminal awareness as an ephemeral surface on kaval

feature · seedling ·proposed ·JUN 19, 2026

A clean-slate design for kolu terminal awareness (git · PR · agent · foreground). Repeat the kaval decoupling one level up — an ephemeral daemon on top of kaval that runs the provider DAG and exposes the provider-authored slice of terminalMetadata as one @kolu/surface collection. Composed in-process for local terminals, served + mirrored + folded for remote ones. It ships standalone first (awarenessd + awareness-tui, the kaval-tui move one level up) and only then does kolu adopt it.

Contents

  • The core model
  • One contract, two wirings — and why remote needs a fold
  • The standalone tool — awarenessd + awareness-tui
  • Four shippable slices
  • Load-bearing complexity & open decisions
  • Status

kaval decoupled the PTY out of kolu into a standalone, minimal, durable daemon with a clean contract — independently shippable, independently driven by kaval-tui. This note does the same move one level up, for the part kolu kept tangling: terminal awareness (git branch/dirty, PR status, agent detection, foreground). A new ephemeral daemon — awarenessd — sits on top of kaval, runs the provider DAG, and exposes its result as one typed @kolu/surface collection. Because a surface can be either composed in-process or served over a link, the same contract has two wirings chosen by where the terminal lives — and kolu becomes a pure consumer of awareness, never the place it tangles.

The core model

One thesis: awareness is a derived surface, and the thing that produces it is a standalone daemon on top of kaval. Three claims follow, each verified against the shipped code.

  • The unit is the provider-authored slice, not the whole record. awarenessd exposes Collection<TerminalId, AwarenessValue> where AwarenessValue is exactly the fields the providers compute — cwd · git · lastAgentCommand · lastActivityAt (server-persisted) plus pr · agent · foreground (live). That is a strict .pick of existing, partition-tested schemas: zero new schema work. It deliberately excludes location (no provider authors it; a remote tool can’t know its own kolu-side hostId) and every client/UI field (themeName, intent, canvasLayout, …) — those are kolu-owned and unproducible host-side.
  • kaval stays byte-for-byte minimal. awarenessd dials kaval as a ptyHostSurface client for the four taps + getScreenText; it adds zero awareness/git/gh logic to kaval. The decoupling is additive — a layer above, never a fork.
  • Ephemerality is the simplifier. Unlike a PTY, awareness never has to survive a restart — it’s re-derivable from live taps + current host fs. So awarenessd sheds all of kaval’s durability machinery: no single-instance lock, no spawn/cleanup (it owns no PTYs), no persisted list (it borrows kaval’s terminal.list), no adoption or reconnect-survival. Every (re)start just re-runs the DAG and recomputes from now.

The payoff: clients keep reading the one terminalMetadata collection they always did. The awareness surface is an internal seam, not a new thing the browser learns.

One contract, two wirings — and why remote needs a fold

The same surface contract is wired two ways, selected by locality. This is the honest version of “local vs remote is only the transport” — with one correction the design panel forced.

  • Local → compose, in-process. The provider DAG runs inside kolu-server exactly as it does today, mutating the terminal’s meta in place and publishing terminalMetadata. The daemon’s surface is implementSurface’d and consumed via directLink — which only formalizes the call boundary that already exists. No socket, no serialization, no fold. And local must stay this way: the in-process DAG is always the running build, so awareness is current by construction.
  • Remote → serve, mirror, fold. awarenessd is Nix-provisioned over ssh (the same staleKey recipe kaval already uses), runs the identical DAG, and serves the slice over stdioLink. kolu-server mirrors it with the already-shipped mirrorRemoteCollection, then folds each upsert into the terminal’s terminalMetadata entry.

The mirror is a fold, not a write — and that's structural, not incidental

terminalMetadata’s entry is one mutable object that publishes the whole record — server fields and kolu-owned client fields and the host-stamped location. So an arriving awareness slice can never whole-value-replace the entry (that would clobber the client’s theme/layout and the location). It must be merged in and split across the persisted-vs-live fence: the live subset (pr · agent · foreground) through the quiet mutator, the persisted subset (cwd · git · lastAgentCommand · lastActivityAt) through the one that arms autosave. Lose that split and you either re-arm the ~150 ms agent firehose (a perf regression) or silently stop persisting. This fold is irreducible domain logic — it exists because terminalMetadata is co-owned, and no generic mirror can absorb it.

So the asymmetry is encapsulated, not erased. Local is a closure-mutate; remote is closure → frame → serve → mirror → fold. They share the invariant provider DAG and one schema — but they are two paths, and pretending otherwise hides the one genuinely-new artifact (the host-side hooks impl that reifies each mutate closure into a serialized slice frame — a wire carries values, not closures). location is stamped kolu-side from the dial-list at the fold.

No @kolu/surface change is required. The compose-or-serve duality already ships end-to-end and is proven by the remote-process-monitor example (define the surface once, implement it twice — directLink in-process, stdioLink over ssh, mirrorRemoteCollection to mirror). A future graduation — mirrorRemoteSurface, the spec-driven dual of implementSurface that drives every primitive of a served surface into a local ctx — is attractive electricity (the demo would delete its hand-rolled bridge loop), but it is not on the critical path and it does not dissolve the fold.

The standalone tool — awarenessd + awareness-tui

Like kaval, the daemon is a deliverable in its own right, shipped and proven before kolu touches it. Where kaval-tui shows what’s running in each PTY, awareness-tui shows what each terminal is in — a host-local “what is every agent doing, across every repo” dashboard with zero kolu-server dependency, dialable over ssh against a prod box.

awareness-tui · ssh nix@prod
$ awareness-tui list --host prod
ID BRANCH DIRTY PR AGENT FOREGROUND
a3f1 feat/dial-ssh ● #1412 ✓ claude · working node
b7c2 master · — codex · waiting codex
c9d4 fix/fold ● #1408 ✗ — nvim
# derived intelligence over the raw PTY — and a future TUI-kolu consumes the same daemon

It ships self-contained: @kolu/terminal-providers already imports nothing from kolu-server, and the host runtime deps are just node · git · gh (SQLite via Node’s built-in node:sqlite — no native addon), so the bin travels cleanly over ssh. Defining the awareness contract once yields all three consumers off one schema: the daemon, the TUI, and kolu-server’s two wirings.

Four shippable slices

Each slice ships and is provable on its own; the long pole is P1 → P2 → P3.

PhaseKindShipsRetires the risk of…
P1featurethe awareness contract (the .pick, defined once) + awarenessd (dials kaval, runs the DAG, serves the slice) + awareness-tui — zero kolu-server involvementdoes the DAG run as a separate, kaval-dialing process and produce correct awareness with no in-process handle? Proven standalone, ssh-shippable, before kolu depends on it — a failure here is cheap and isolated
P2refactorkolu-server composes the surface in-process for local terminals (directLink over the existing DAG), folding through the existing mutatorsproves the fold mechanics (merge into the co-owned entry, split across the persisted/live fence) on the safe in-process path — debugged before any wire exists
P3featureNix-provision awarenessd over ssh; kolu-server serves + mirrors + folds for remote terminals; the host hooks-impl reifies closures → tagged frames; a meta.version handshake gates incompatible hosts into re-provisionthe genuinely-new artifacts in isolation — reification, the fence across the wire, version-skew — with local awareness (P2) safe from regression
P4refactor(optional) graduate mirrorRemoteSurface as the spec-driven dual of implementSurface; the awareness mirror and remote-process-monitor both consume itper-surface hand-mirroring for every future served surface — pure decomplecting, strictly additive, off the critical path

Load-bearing complexity & open decisions

The cleanest design still carries four irreducible costs — all remote-only, none dissolvable, and that confinement is the point:

  1. Closure → frame reification — the one genuinely-new artifact: the host hooks-impl applies each mutate to its own record, then emits the resulting slice value as a snapshot/delta frame. The DAG is untouched.
  2. The fold + persisted/live fence, re-applied kolu-side on every remote upsert (see the callout). Frames must carry the split, or it degrades to runtime discipline.
  3. Freshness / semantic version-skew — the deepest. In-process local providers are always current; a remote host runs a provisioned, possibly-stale binary. A version handshake catches shape skew, not semantic drift (a new agent state an old parser never emits). Bounded by re-provision-on-dial + ephemerality, but only re-provisioning truly closes it — which is why local must never route through the daemon.
  4. location stamped kolu-side at the fold — a small permanent special-case at the seam.

(Cold-start recompute storms and a two-hop key-set lag are operational, not architectural — bounded by idle-gating and the existing pr:pending / agent:null seed.)

Open decisions (defaults in bold): the persisted/live split is carried by a static field→half map applied kolu-side (thinner wire) vs per-frame tags; one awarenessd per host (mirrors kaval’s per-host socket, maximizes the dashboard) vs per-session; staleKey-gated re-provision (kaval’s existing behavior) vs always-on-dial for the tightest semantic currency; build mirrorRemoteSurface when a second served-surface consumer appears vs now.

Status

Proposed — a clean-slate replacement for the awareness layer, child of kaval-sessions. It supersedes the earlier from-scratch plan ( #1398 , closed) and the in-process provider extraction ( #1409 , closed): both kept awareness inside kolu and split hairs over an in-process compose-vs-serve boundary that this design dissolves by moving the producer into a daemon. Built from an adversarial, cost-blind design panel (verdict: sound, with the asymmetry-is-encapsulated and mirror-is-a-fold corrections folded in above), grounded by reading the shipped code: @kolu/terminal-providers, mirrorRemoteCollection, the remote-process-monitor “define once, implement twice” demo, and kaval’s provisioning recipe. The substrate it stands on — kaval (untouched), location as a first-class discriminated union — is already merged. The long pole is P1 → P2 → P3, and P1 (the standalone awarenessd + awareness-tui) is the recommended first move: independently useful, and it retires the biggest risk before kolu depends on a line of it.
